acceptable_use_policy: Data is used for law enforcement purposes only. Data is owned by Oceanside CA PD and is never sold to 3rd parties.
prohibited_uses: Immigration enforcement, traffic enforcement, harassment or intimidation, usage based solely on a protected class (i.e. race, sex, religion), Personal use.
access_policy: All system access requires a valid reason and is stored indefinitely.
hotlist_policy: A hotlist is a list of license plates associated with active police investigations including: stolen vehicles, stolen plates, and vehicles associated with missing persons or criminal investigations. Hotlist hits are required to be human verified prior to any enforcement action. Once the vehicle is no longer needed for the police investigation, the license plate is removed from the hotlist.

overview: Oceanside CA PD uses Flock Safety technology to capture objective evidence without compromising on individual privacy. Oceanside CA PD utilizes retroactive search to solve crimes after they've occurred. Additionally, Oceanside CA PD utilizes real time alerting of hotlist vehicles to capture wanted criminals. In an effort to ensure proper usage and guardrails are in place, they have made the below policies and usage statistics available to the public.
policy_info:
The Oceanside Police Department Automated License Plate Reader (ALPR) Policy is available here:

https://www.oceansidepolice.com/transparency/policies-procedures/-folder-559#docfold_14_2171_4130_559
california_svs:
Distinct Vehicles (No Duplicates) detected in the last 30 days

604,976
sb54: No data shall be shared in a manner that contradicts California Senate Bill 54. Sharing ALPR data with other law enforcement or prosecutorial agencies shall be consistent with California SB54. The Oceanside Police Department does not permit license plate reader data to be shared with the Department of Homeland Security or Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) agents for the use of immigration enforcement.
